title: refactor(machine,wsl): improve operations of Windows API
body:
1. Prefer using the built-in functions of `golang.org/x/sys/windows` rather than using `syscall` to directly call system APIs
2. Reduce bitwise operations to improve code maintainability
3. Minimize defining your own Flags, and prioritize using those already defined in `golang.org/x/sys/windows`

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM but I am not a windows programmer.
@n1hility @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@ l0rd PTAL
@baude PTAL. Code LGTM but again not the most familiar with windows.
would love a sniff from @n1hility here but not required imho.
Please separate style changes from actual fixes.

> Please separate style changes from actual fixes.

Done

@BlackHole1 since you are thinking of enhancing this code (e.g. console work)/ I just want to mention that the dynamic wsl installation behavior is legacy behavior. It might end up getting disabled or removed at some point now that there is Hyper-V and WSL installation elements in the installer. We'd still take the change if you were interested, but just want to make sure you don't feel like we are dropping your work right after you make it.
The rest of the changes (other than the ones I commented on) on look good to me

> @BlackHole1 since you are thinking of enhancing this code (e.g. console work)/ I just want to mention that the dynamic wsl installation behavior is legacy behavior. It might end up getting disabled or removed at some point now that there is Hyper-V and WSL installation elements in the installer. We'd still take the change if you were interested, but just want to make sure you don't feel like we are dropping your work right after you make it.

Thank you for letting me know :)
I'm not sure if I should continue now. As you just said, this piece of code might be deleted/disabled in the future, so optimizing the readability of this code isn't very cost-effective. Especially since, as you mentioned before, it requires testing on all Windows versions that support WSL and Hyper-V, which greatly reduces the cost-effectiveness, making it almost unnecessary. Do you have any thoughts?

> BTW. If this feature is deleted/disabled, relying solely on the installation package (wix) to handle the WSL/Hyper-V environment. Then, if the user manually disables the feature or deletes WSL in some situations, is Podman not prepared to handle such similar situations?
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *BlackHole1* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Thank you for letting me know :) I'm not sure if I should continue now. As you just said, this piece of code might be deleted/disabled in the future, so optimizing the readability of this code isn't very cost-effective. Especially since, as you mentioned before, it requires testing on all Windows versions that support WSL and Hyper-V, which greatly reduces the cost-effectiveness, making it almost unnecessary. Do you have any thoughts?

So most of these changes seem low risk to me, it's really just the elevated aspect I was uncertain of. Some of these routines will likely still have a use but the relaunch code is unlikely to last long so I dont think evolving that portion of the code further beyond your patch set is worth additional effort that you would probably rather go towards other interesting problems / features.
@n1hility I understand, I have rolled back the permission checking code and retained the remaining changes. Please rereview.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 0c587f0246cd539b15cccaeb77d1fd47fe69203b. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Same error as in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22953#issuecomment-2160536827
Friendly ping :) @n1hility
LGTM Thanks for the patch!

The [rawhide test](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/d874e7b6-17c3-47d7-b2fe-422dadbc1a69/) failed in the pod creation test, with a mouthful of an error message:

That's obviously unrelated, and feels like a flake (i.e. only happens sometimes). So I guess it can be ignored here, but perhaps it rings a bell?
